revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 健武山神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那須郡}} |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]健武2302 |地図 = |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]、[[金山彦神|金山彦尊]] |社格 = 郷社、式内社 |創建 = [[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''健武山神社'''（たけぶやまじんじゃ）…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 健武山神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那須郡}} |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]健武2302 |地図 = |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]、[[金山彦神|金山彦尊]] |社格 = 郷社、式内社 |創建 = [[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''健武山神社'''（たけぶやまじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[806年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年）に創建された。[[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]でもある<ref name="散歩">栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』山川出版社、2007年、73-74p</ref>。
[[中世]]においては当地の領主だった[[武茂氏]]の保護を受けていた。[[江戸時代]]は[[修験者]]が管理していたが、[[水戸藩]]第9代[[藩主]][[徳川斉昭]]により、[[唯一神道]]に改められた<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
かつての[[祭神]]は[[スサノオ|素戔嗚命]]であったが、現在は[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]と[[金山彦神|金山彦尊]]である<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
== 文化財 == *棟札（那珂川町指定文化財 昭和43年3月13日指定）<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）]那珂川町</ref>
== 交通アクセス == *[[矢板インターチェンジ|矢板IC]]より車15分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』山川出版社、2007年
{{Pref-stub|pref=栃木県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふやましんしや}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Takebuyama Shrine'''|健武山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shimotsuke Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takebuyama Shrine'''|健武山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shimotsuke Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takebuyama Shrine'''|健武山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shimotsuke Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takebuyama Shrine'''|健武山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shimotsuke Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takebuyama Shrine'''|健武山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shimotsuke Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takebuyama Shrine'''|健武山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shimotsuke Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Takebuyama Shrine | native_name = 健武山神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Takebuyama Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.749826|140.19477}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Takebuyama Shrine'''|健武山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shimotsuke Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Takebuyama Shrine | native_name = 健武山神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Takebuyama Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.749826|140.19477}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Takebuyama Shrine'''|健武山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shimotsuke Province]].

revision.comment: 4 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 健武山神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那須郡}} |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]健武2302 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]、[[金山彦神|金山彦尊]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=健武山神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''健武山神社'''（たけぶやまじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[806年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年）に創建された。[[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]でもある<ref name="散歩">栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』山川出版社、2007年、73-74p</ref>。
[[中世]]においては当地の領主だった[[武茂氏]]の保護を受けていた。[[江戸時代]]は[[修験者]]が管理していたが、[[水戸藩]]第9代[[藩主]][[徳川斉昭]]により、[[唯一神道]]に改められた<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
かつての[[祭神]]は[[スサノオ|素戔嗚命]]であったが、現在は[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]と[[金山彦神|金山彦尊]]である<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
== 文化財 == *棟札（那珂川町指定文化財 昭和43年3月13日指定）<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）]那珂川町</ref>
== 交通アクセス == *[[矢板インターチェンジ|矢板IC]]より車15分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』[[山川出版社]]、2007年
{{Pref-stub|pref=栃木県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふやましんしや}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:那珂川町の建築物]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]] [[Category:宇都宮氏|社]] [[Category:徳川斉昭]]

{{神社 |名称 = 健武山神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那須郡}} |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]健武2302 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]、[[金山彦神|金山彦尊]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=健武山神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''健武山神社'''（たけぶやまじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[806年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年）に創建された。[[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]でもある<ref name="散歩">栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』山川出版社、2007年、73-74p</ref>。
[[中世]]においては当地の領主だった[[武茂氏]]の保護を受けていた。[[江戸時代]]は[[修験者]]が管理していたが、[[水戸藩]]第9代[[藩主]][[徳川斉昭]]により、[[唯一神道]]に改められた<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
かつての[[祭神]]は[[スサノオ|素戔嗚命]]であったが、現在は[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]と[[金山彦神|金山彦尊]]である<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
== 文化財 == *棟札（那珂川町指定文化財 昭和43年3月13日指定）<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）]那珂川町</ref>
== 交通アクセス == *[[矢板インターチェンジ|矢板IC]]より車15分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』[[山川出版社]]、2007年

{{translated page|ja|健武山神社|version=107317695|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 健武山神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那須郡}} |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]健武2302 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]、[[金山彦神|金山彦尊]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=健武山神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''健武山神社'''（たけぶやまじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[806年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年）に創建された。[[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]でもある<ref name="散歩">栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』山川出版社、2007年、73-74p</ref>。
[[中世]]においては当地の領主だった[[武茂氏]]の保護を受けていた。[[江戸時代]]は[[修験者]]が管理していたが、[[水戸藩]]第9代[[藩主]][[徳川斉昭]]により、[[唯一神道]]に改められた<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
かつての[[祭神]]は[[スサノオ|素戔嗚命]]であったが、現在は[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]と[[金山彦神|金山彦尊]]である<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
== 文化財 == *棟札（那珂川町指定文化財 昭和43年3月13日指定）<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）]那珂川町</ref>
== 交通アクセス == *[[矢板インターチェンジ|矢板IC]]より車15分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』[[山川出版社]]、2007年
{{Pref-stub|pref=栃木県}} {{shinto-stub}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:那珂川町の建築物]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]] [[Category:宇都宮氏|社]] [[Category:徳川斉昭]]

{{wikidata link|Q119929432}} [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|健武山神社|version=107317695|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 健武山神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那須郡}} |所在地 = {{jalink|栃木県|Q44843}}{{jalink|那須郡|Q1203347}}{{jalink|那珂川町|Q44603}}健武2302 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ヤマトタケル|Q461258|日本武尊}}、{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦尊}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社}} |創建 = {{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=健武山神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''健武山神社'''（たけぶやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|栃木県|Q44843}}{{jalink|那須郡|Q1203347}}{{jalink|那珂川町|Q44603}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。
== 歴史 == {{jalink|806年|Q30021}}（{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年）に創建された。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}でもある<ref name="散歩">栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』山川出版社、2007年、73-74p</ref>。
{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}においては当地の領主だった{{jalink|武茂氏|Q11546315}}の保護を受けていた。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は{{jalink|修験者}}が管理していたが、{{jalink|水戸藩|Q1146904}}第9代{{jalink|藩主}}{{jalink|徳川斉昭|Q1323770}}により、{{jalink|唯一神道}}に改められた<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
かつての{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素戔嗚命}}であったが、現在は{{jalink|ヤマトタケル|Q461258|日本武尊}}と{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦尊}}である<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
== 文化財 == *棟札（那珂川町指定文化財 昭和43年3月13日指定）<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）]那珂川町</ref>
== 交通アクセス == *{{jalink|矢板インターチェンジ|Q11584145|矢板IC}}より車15分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』{{jalink|山川出版社|Q11468274}}、2007年
{{Pref-stub|pref=栃木県}} {{shinto-stub}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:那珂川町の建築物]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]] [[Category:宇都宮氏|社]] [[Category:徳川斉昭]]

'''健武山神社'''（たけぶやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|栃木県|Q44843}}{{jalink|那須郡|Q1203347}}{{jalink|那珂川町|Q44603}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}（{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年）に創建された。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}でもある<ref name="散歩">栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』山川出版社、2007年、73-74p</ref>。

{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}においては当地の領主だった{{jalink|武茂氏|Q11546315}}の保護を受けていた。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は{{jalink|修験者}}が管理していたが、{{jalink|水戸藩|Q1146904}}第9代{{jalink|藩主}}{{jalink|徳川斉昭|Q1323770}}により、{{jalink|唯一神道}}に改められた<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

かつての{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素戔嗚命}}であったが、現在は{{jalink|ヤマトタケル|Q461258|日本武尊}}と{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦尊}}である<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

=== 文化財 ===
*棟札（那珂川町指定文化財 昭和43年3月13日指定）<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）]那珂川町</ref>

=== 交通アクセス ===
*{{jalink|矢板インターチェンジ|Q11584145|矢板IC}}より車15分。

=== 脚注 ===

{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}においては当地の領主だった{{jalink|武茂氏|Q11546315}}の保護を受けていた。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は{{jalink|修験者}}が管理していたが、{{jalink|水戸藩|Q1146904}}第9代{{jalink|藩主}}{{jalink|徳川斉昭|Q1323770}}により、{{jalink|唯一神道}}に改められた<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

かつての{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素戔嗚命}}であったが、現在は{{jalink|ヤマトタケル|Q461258|日本武尊}}と{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦尊}}である<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

=== 文化財 ===
*棟札（那珂川町指定文化財 昭和43年3月13日指定）<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）]那珂川町</ref>

'''健武山神社'''（たけぶやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|栃木県|Q44843}}{{jalink|那須郡|Q1203347}}{{jalink|那珂川町|Q44603}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}（{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年）に創建された。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}でもある<ref name="散歩">栃木県歴史散歩編集委員会 編『栃木県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩9）』山川出版社、2007年、73-74p</ref>。

{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}においては当地の領主だった{{jalink|武茂氏|Q11546315}}の保護を受けていた。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は{{jalink|修験者}}が管理していたが、{{jalink|水戸藩|Q1146904}}第9代{{jalink|藩主}}{{jalink|徳川斉昭|Q1323770}}により、{{jalink|唯一神道}}に改められた<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

かつての{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素戔嗚命}}であったが、現在は{{jalink|ヤマトタケル|Q461258|日本武尊}}と{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦尊}}である<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

=== 文化財 ===
*棟札（那珂川町指定文化財 昭和43年3月13日指定）<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）]那珂川町</ref>

=== 交通アクセス ===
*{{jalink|矢板インターチェンジ|Q11584145|矢板IC}}より車15分。

|社格   = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603}}, {{jalink|Nasu District|Q1203347}}, {{jalink|Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{jalink|806|Q30021}} ({{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it was protected by the {{jalink|Takemu clan|Q11546315}}, local lords of the area. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was managed by {{jalink|shugenja}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{jalink|Mito Domain|Q1146904}}, {{jalink|Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770}}, it was changed to {{jalink|Yuiitsu Shinto}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} was {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|Yaita IC}}.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

=== References ===
* Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', {{jalink|Yamakawa Publishing|Q11468274}}, 2007

'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603}}, {{jalink|Nasu District|Q1203347}}, {{jalink|Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{jalink|806|Q30021}} ({{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it was protected by the {{jalink|Takemu clan|Q11546315}}, local lords of the area. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was managed by {{jalink|shugenja}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{jalink|Mito Domain|Q1146904}}, {{jalink|Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770}}, it was changed to {{jalink|Yuiitsu Shinto}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} was {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|Yaita IC}}.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

=== References ===
* Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', {{jalink|Yamakawa Publishing|Q11468274}}, 2007

'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603}}, {{jalink|Nasu District|Q1203347}}, {{jalink|Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{jalink|806|Q30021}} ({{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it was protected by the {{jalink|Takemu clan|Q11546315}}, local lords of the area. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was managed by {{jalink|shugenja}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{jalink|Mito Domain|Q1146904}}, {{jalink|Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770}}, it was changed to {{jalink|Yuiitsu Shinto}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} was {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|Yaita IC}}.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

=== References ===
* Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', {{jalink|Yamakawa Publishing|Q11468274}}, 2007

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}

'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603}}, {{jalink|Nasu District|Q1203347}}, {{jalink|Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{jalink|806|Q30021}} ({{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it was protected by the {{jalink|Takemu clan|Q11546315}}, local lords of the area. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was managed by {{jalink|shugenja}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{jalink|Mito Domain|Q1146904}}, {{jalink|Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770}}, it was changed to {{jalink|Yuiitsu Shinto}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} was {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|Yaita IC}}.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

=== References ===
* Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', {{jalink|Yamakawa Publishing|Q11468274}}, 2007

{{shinto-stub}}

'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603|WD=Q44603}}, {{ill|lt=Nasu District|Q1203347|WD=Q1203347}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Takemu clan|Q11546315|WD=Q11546315}}, local lords of the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was managed by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shugenja|1=UNKNOWN}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{ill|lt=Mito Domain|Q1146904|WD=Q1146904}}, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770|WD=Q1323770}}, it was changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuiitsu Shinto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|WD=Q11584145|lt=Yaita IC}}.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

=== References ===

'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603|WD=Q44603}}, {{ill|lt=Nasu District|Q1203347|WD=Q1203347}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Takemu clan|Q11546315|WD=Q11546315}}, local lords of the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was managed by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shugenja|1=UNKNOWN}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{ill|lt=Mito Domain|Q1146904|WD=Q1146904}}, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770|WD=Q1323770}}, it was changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuiitsu Shinto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603|WD=Q44603|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Nasu District|Q1203347|WD=Q1203347|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Takemu clan|Q11546315|WD=Q11546315|1=Takemu clan}}, local lords of the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was managed by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shugenja|1=UNKNOWN}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{ill|lt=Mito Domain|Q1146904|WD=Q1146904|1=Mito Domain}}, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770|WD=Q1323770|1=Tokugawa Nariaki}}, it was changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuiitsu Shinto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|WD=Q11584145|lt=Yaita IC|1=Yaita interchange}}.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

=== References ===

Its former {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|WD=Q11584145|lt=Yaita IC|1=Yaita interchange}}.

'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603|WD=Q44603|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Nasu District|Q1203347|WD=Q1203347|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Takemu clan|Q11546315|WD=Q11546315|1=Takemu clan}}, local lords of the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was managed by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shugenja|1=UNKNOWN}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{ill|lt=Mito Domain|Q1146904|WD=Q1146904|1=Mito Domain}}, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770|WD=Q1323770|1=Tokugawa Nariaki}}, it was changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuiitsu Shinto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|WD=Q11584145|lt=Yaita IC|1=Yaita interchange}}.

'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603|WD=Q44603|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Nasu District|Q1203347|WD=Q1203347|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Takemu clan|Q11546315|WD=Q11546315|1=Takemu clan}}, local lords of the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was managed by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shugenja|1=UNKNOWN}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{ill|lt=Mito Domain|Q1146904|WD=Q1146904|1=Mito Domain}}, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770|WD=Q1323770|1=Tokugawa Nariaki}}, it was changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuiitsu Shinto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|WD=Q11584145|lt=Yaita IC|1=Yaita interchange}}.

'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603|WD=Q44603|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Nasu District|Q1203347|WD=Q1203347|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Takemu clan|Q11546315|WD=Q11546315|1=Takemu clan}}, local lords of the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was managed by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shugenja|1=UNKNOWN}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{ill|lt=Mito Domain|Q1146904|WD=Q1146904|1=Mito Domain}}, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770|WD=Q1323770|1=Tokugawa Nariaki}}, it was changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuiitsu Shinto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|WD=Q11584145|lt=Yaita IC|1=Yaita interchange}}.

=== Notes ===

'''Takebuyama Shrine''' (たけぶやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|Q44603|WD=Q44603|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Nasu District|Q1203347|WD=Q1203347|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It was founded in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1). It is also a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}<ref name="散歩">Editorial Committee for Historical Walks in Tochigi (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Tochigi Prefecture (Historical Walk 9)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2007, pp.73-74</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Takemu clan|Q11546315|WD=Q11546315|1=Takemu clan}}, local lords of the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was managed by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shugenja|1=UNKNOWN}}, but under the 9th lord of the {{ill|lt=Mito Domain|Q1146904|WD=Q1146904|1=Mito Domain}}, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Nariaki|Q1323770|WD=Q1323770|1=Tokugawa Nariaki}}, it was changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuiitsu Shinto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

Its former {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, but now they are {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Cultural properties ===
* Ridge tag (Nakagawa Town designated cultural property, designated March 13, 1968)<ref name="町">[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1222-1202-52.html 町指定文化財（有形文化財No2）] Nakagawa Town</ref>

=== Access ===
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yaita Interchange|Q11584145|WD=Q11584145|lt=Yaita IC|1=Yaita interchange}}.
